articles.title: Saudi Arabia and its allies must curb the growing Israeli-Emirati axis
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia and its allies must curb the growing Israeli-Emirati axis US President Donald Trump has hit a brick wall with  Iran .  Whatever path he now takes, and whoever was stupid enough to follow him, will lead them into more danger. If Trump opts for war, he can be sure it will be more costly in  US  lives than the first round was.  If US Marines land on any of Iran’s islands in the Strait of Hormuz, they would  become sitting ducks  for drones and missiles on terrain that gives them no cover. articles.title: Lebanon death toll rises to 2,696 as Israeli attacks continue
articles.summary: Lebanon death toll rises to 2,696 as Israeli attacks continue The number of people killed in Israel’s war on Lebanon since 2 March has climbed to 2,696, with 8,264 others wounded, Lebanon's official National News Agency reported. Israeli air strikes killed several civilians on Monday as warplanes carried out intensive raids across towns in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Fed’s Williams Sees Rates Well Positioned Amid Iran War Uncertainty

articles.title: Trump pleads with South Korea to join US mission to protect Gulf shipping
articles.summary: Trump pleads with South Korea to join US mission to protect Gulf shipping US President Donald Trump has called on South Korea to help secure shipping routes near Iran. Trump said Washington is seeking broader international participation to bail the US from what has become another war of choice in the Middle East. He added that US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth will hold a news conference on Tuesday alongside General Dan Caine to provide further details on the operation.

articles.title: Oil surges past $115 after reported drone strike on UAE port
articles.summary: Oil surges past $115 after reported drone strike on UAE port Oil prices continue their upward incline, with Brent crude climbing to fresh intraday highs after reports of a drone strike targeting a key oil facility in the United Arab Emirates. Brent rose above $115 a barrel shortly after 17:00 BST, gaining more than five percent on the day, following reports that the port of Fujairah had come under attack. Located on the UAE’s eastern coast, outside the Strait of Hormuz, Fujairah serves as a critical export point. A pipeline from Abu Dhabi’s oil fields feeds the terminal, allowing shipments to

articles.title: Iran says managing Strait of Hormuz is its ‘legitimate right’
articles.summary: Iran says managing Strait of Hormuz is its ‘legitimate right’ Iranian First Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref has said Tehran has the right to control activity in the Strait of Hormuz, according to the ISNA. Aref said managing the vital waterway is Iran’s “legitimate right”, as tensions continue to centre on the corridor that carries a large share of global energy supplies. He added that Iran is not seeking conflict but will respond forcefully if it is drawn into one. articles.title: US says it destroyed Iranian boats, Iran denies naval losses
articles.summary: US says it destroyed Iranian boats, Iran denies naval losses The US says its forces have destroyed six Iranian small boats and intercepted cruise missiles and drones during a new operation aimed at securing shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian officials rejected the US account. State television quoted a military official denying that any Iranian boats had been sunk, directly contradicting the US claims. US Admiral Brad Cooper, the head of Central Command, said on Monday that US forces carried out the action as part of efforts to keep the waterway open amid escalating tensions.

articles.summary: Italy probes ‘abduction’ after Israel intercepts Gaza aid flotilla Prosecutors in Rome have opened an abduction investigation after Israeli forces intercepted 22 boats from the Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters near Crete, according to Italian news agency ANSA. Israel abducted 175 activists from the vessels. Most were later released, including 24 Italian nationals, while two - Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avila - were taken into custody in Ashkelon, a city in southern Israel near the Gaza border. articles.summary: UAE says it intercepted three missiles from Iran UAE’s defence ministry has said that four missiles were launched from Iran towards the Emirates, three of which were intercepted and one which fell into the sea.  This is the first attack from Iran since the US-Iran ceasefire began.

articles.title: UAE issues new missile alert
articles.summary: UAE issues new missile alert The United Arab Emirates National Emergency and Crisis and Disaster Management Authority has sent out a warning that its air defence systems are responding to missile threat.  A similar alert urging residents in Dubai to take shelter was issued earlier on Monday, the first such alert since the US-Iran ceasefire, before a second message confirmed that the situation was “now safe”.
